brands.name: Subway
brands.notes: World's largest sandwich franchise (founded 1965, franchising since 1974) with a low entry cost ($15K fee) but high combined ongoing fees (12.5% of gross) and a multi-year US unit contraction. Franchise recruitment runs through subwayfranchise.com; no published list of open US markets was found.

riskFlags.title: Sustained US unit contraction
riskFlags.detail: Subway closed a net 729 US stores in 2025, its steepest decline since 2021, and has shrunk by 3,417 US restaurants since early 2021 (22,190 to 18,773). Franchise revenue fell 6% in 2025 as royalties declined.

riskFlags.title: Weak unit economics vs. sandwich competitors; franchisee bankruptcies
riskFlags.detail: Subway's AUV is estimated at roughly $490K-$500K, far below Jersey Mike's (~$1.3M-$1.4M) and Jimmy John's (~$1M), pressuring franchisee profitability; several operators have declared bankruptcy, including 43-unit MTF Enterprises in 2026.

riskFlags.title: Franchisee association disputes over discount promotions
riskFlags.detail: Franchisees have repeatedly clashed with corporate over mandated value promotions ($5/$6.99 Footlong offers), including FTC complaints alleging they were 'bullied into honoring a promotion that is unprofitable.' The North American Association of Subway Franchisees (NAASF, representing ~10,000 North American locations) said discounting is 'killing our bottom line.'

riskFlags.title: High combined ongoing fees (12.5% of gross sales)
riskFlags.detail: 8% royalty plus 4.5% advertising is among the highest combined fee loads in QSR, a heavy burden on top of below-average unit volumes.

riskFlags.title: No Item 19 financial performance representation in the FDD
riskFlags.detail: Subway does not disclose AUV or earnings data in its FDD, forcing prospective franchisees to rely on third-party estimates (~$490K AUV per third-party analyses) and independent validation.

liquidCapitalMin.note: $100,000 liquid assets (cash-on-hand) required per location, per franchisor FAQ

totalInvestmentMin.note: Entrepreneur range $238,625-$536,745; Franchise Direct (2025 FDD) shows a $199,135 low that includes non-traditional formats

totalUnits.note: Global figure (Entrepreneur, -3% trend); ~18,773 US locations per Restaurant Dive (May 2026)

closureCount.note: Net US closures in 2025; 3,417 net US closures since early 2021

minFootprint.note: Non-traditional formats start at ~400 sq ft per franchisor site; traditional storefronts are larger

fddAvailable.note: 2025 FDD summarized publicly by Franchise Direct

item19Available.note: Subway does not make an Item 19 financial performance representation; third-party AUV estimates run ~$490K-$500K (Circana via Restaurant Dive)

veteranDiscount.note: $7,500 off first-unit franchise fee for veterans

multiUnitAvailable.note: Multi-unit packages of 25, 50, and 100+ locations offered

exclusiveTerritories.note: Conflict: 2025 FDD summary states 'Franchisees will not receive an exclusive territory'; Entrepreneur's listing says exclusive territories are available. FDD-derived source treated as authoritative.

isGrowing.note: US unit count declining since 2021; Entrepreneur shows -3% total units

brands.name: KFC
brands.notes: Yum! Brands fried-chicken giant (founded 1930, franchising since 1952) with strong global growth but a multi-year US sales and traffic slide; entry costs are heavy ($1.85M-$3.77M, $1.5M net worth) and franchise opportunities are listed across all 50 states.

riskFlags.title: Multi-decade US sales slide
riskFlags.detail: KFC's US systemwide sales fell about 5% in the first half of 2025 while international sales rose 7%; the chain logged quarterly US traffic declines of 2%-12% through 2024-2025 and closed more than 3% of its US restaurants.

riskFlags.title: Franchisee distress and closures
riskFlags.detail: Major Midwestern franchisee EYM Chicken closed about 25 KFC restaurants across Illinois, Indiana and Wisconsin (more than half its 47 stores) amid bankruptcy restructuring in August 2024.

riskFlags.title: Lost US chicken category leadership
riskFlags.detail: Once the top US chicken chain, KFC now ranks third behind Chick-fil-A and Popeyes on most industry rankings, reflecting sustained competitive share loss at home.

riskFlags.title: Large international franchisee collapse (Turkey, 2025)
riskFlags.detail: Yum Brands severed ties with major international franchisee IS Gida in 2025 for failing to meet standards; IS Gida declared bankruptcy and 283 Turkish locations closed. International event, but illustrates concentration risk among mega-franchisees.

nationwide.note: Listing shows KFC franchise opportunities available in all 50 US states plus DC

royaltyPercent.note: Entrepreneur lists 4%-5% (lower bound shown); most FDD-based reviews cite 5% of gross sales

avgUnitRevenue.note: 2025 FDD Item 19: average net sales of 2,850 franchised single-brand US outlets open all of FY2024; median $1,283,574; gross sales, not profit; only 44% of units met or exceeded the average

totalUnits.note: Global figure; US footprint has been contracting (see riskFlags)

trainingWeeks.note: 6 weeks on-the-job plus 1.5 days classroom; approximately 10 weeks total including orientation and online components

item19Available.note: Item 19 discloses average/median net sales for franchised and company-owned outlets

brands.name: Burger King
brands.notes: Restaurant Brands International's flame-grilled burger chain (founded 1954, franchising since 1961), currently mid-turnaround ('Reclaim the Flame') after a wave of large franchisee bankruptcies in 2023-2025; new franchising runs primarily through multi-unit acquisitions via franchising.bk.com.

riskFlags.title: Wave of large franchisee bankruptcies (2023-2025)
riskFlags.detail: Four major operators filed Chapter 11: TOMS King (90 units, 2023), Meridian Restaurants Unlimited (120 units, 2023), Premier Kings (172 units, 2023), and Consolidated Burger Holdings (57 units, April 2025, after sales fell 12.5% and operating losses doubled to $12.5M).

riskFlags.title: Mandatory remodel capex strains leveraged franchisees
riskFlags.detail: Bankruptcy filings shared a pattern: operators with significant acquisition debt could not simultaneously service obligations and fund mandatory 'Reclaim the Flame' remodels, which the brand has backed with hundreds of millions in corporate investment.
riskFlags.source: Eleven Floor (Consolidated Burger Holdings bankruptcy analysis)

riskFlags.title: Corporate consolidation of franchise base
riskFlags.detail: Parent RBI acquired Burger King's largest franchisee, Carrols Restaurant Group (~1,000+ units), for about $1 billion in 2024, planning to remodel and refranchise units — reshaping who holds territory in the system.

nationwide.note: Official franchising site solicits US inquiries with a 'Priority Markets' and 'Existing Markets' map and states BK offers 'franchising opportunities primarily through multi-unit acquisitions'; no state-level restrictions published

avgUnitRevenue.note: FDD-reported average annual sales for franchised traditional restaurants (2024 data; QSR Magazine corroborates $1.658M franchised / $1.731M company-owned). BK does not make an Item 19 earnings claim — figure is gross sales, not profit. Non-traditional units average $1,321,324.

totalUnits.note: Global figure, +2.5% trend; US base has contracted amid franchisee bankruptcies (see riskFlags)

trainingWeeks.note: Derived: up to 64 classroom hours plus up to 320 on-the-job hours (~8 full-time weeks), held in Miami, FL or a BK-specified location

multiUnitAvailable.note: Franchising opportunities offered 'primarily through multi-unit acquisitions'

isGrowing.note: Global units +2.5% per Entrepreneur; US unit count has declined amid franchisee bankruptcies and closures — see riskFlags

brands.name: Wendy's
brands.notes: Legacy burger brand (founded 1969, franchising since 1971) with strong ~$2.1M franchised unit volumes and official development incentives (Build-to-Suit, Groundbreaker/Pacesetter), but currently executing 'Project Fresh' — closing roughly 300 underperforming US restaurants in 2025-2026 amid same-store sales declines.

riskFlags.title: Project Fresh closures: ~300 US restaurants closing
riskFlags.detail: Wendy's shuttered 28 locations in Q4 2025 and expects to close 5%-6% of its 5,959 US restaurants (298-358 locations) in the first half of 2026, targeting consistently underperforming units as part of its Project Fresh turnaround.

riskFlags.title: US same-store sales declines, underperforming peers
riskFlags.detail: US same-store sales fell 4.7% in Q3 2025 (recent quarters down ~5%-6% year over year), underperforming McDonald's and Burger King; global systemwide sales fell 2.6%.

riskFlags.title: Turnaround program underway
riskFlags.detail: Project Fresh (launched October 2025) is a 4-pillar plan covering brand revitalization, operations, system optimization, and capital reallocation; closures are paired with reinvestment in higher-performing units and new-build incentives.

nationwide.note: Official availability-map page: 'Wendy's is seeking new multi-unit experienced operators...' with a US/global availability map; disclaimer that 'there is no assurance or guarantee that you will be approved for development in any geographic area'

liquidCapitalMin.note: Franchisor requires minimum liquidity of $500,000 and minimum net worth of $1M

totalInvestmentMin.note: Range $393,191-$2,992,000; low end reflects non-traditional/conversion formats

royaltyPercent.note: Standard royalty 4% of gross sales; Entrepreneur lists a 4%-6% range

adFundPercent.note: Standard 4% advertising/marketing fund; Entrepreneur lists 1.5%-4%, reflecting development incentive programs (Groundbreaker/Pacesetter) that reduce fees in early years

avgUnitRevenue.note: Median AUV per FDD Item 19 across ~5,100 US locations (top quartile $2.7M, bottom quartile $1.5M) per FranchiseInvestorData; FranchiseChatter's 2022-FDD review reported $1.89M average sales. Gross sales, not profit.

totalUnits.note: Global figure, +4.2% per Entrepreneur; ~94% franchised. US closures of ~300 units planned for 2026 (see riskFlags)

trainingWeeks.note: Initial training modules take approximately 20-24 weeks, including ~72 classroom hours and extensive on-the-job training (~520+ hours)

item19Available.note: FDD Item 19 unit-volume data publicly cited by multiple FDD-analysis services

multiUnitAvailable.note: Franchisor is 'seeking new multi-unit experienced operators'

exclusiveTerritories.note: 'Wendy's does not provide territory exclusivity for any franchisees'

value: market research and site selection help

brands.name: Domino's
brands.notes: World's largest pizza chain (founded 1960 in Ypsilanti, MI; franchising since 1967) with strong FDD-disclosed unit volumes and low entry costs — but US franchises are currently sold only to internal candidates with at least one year as a Domino's GM or supervisor.

riskFlags.title: Franchise sales closed to external candidates
riskFlags.detail: Per the franchisor: 'At this time, Domino's is only considering internal franchisee candidates. In order to become a franchisee in the U.S., you must have at least one year of experience working as a Domino's general manager or supervisor,' plus completion of Franchise Management School. Outside investors cannot buy in directly.

riskFlags.title: Joint-employer wage-theft litigation (NY Attorney General)
riskFlags.detail: In 2016 the NY AG sued Domino's Pizza entities and three franchisees for systemic wage theft (at least $565K underpaid at 10 stores), alleging Domino's PULSE payroll software under-calculated wages — the first case treating a fast-food franchisor as joint employer; franchisee settlements totaled $480K.
riskFlags.source: New York Attorney General press release

riskFlags.title: No exclusive territory
riskFlags.detail: Franchisees receive only an 'area of primary responsibility' — typically a one-mile radius around the store (half-mile in dense areas) — not an exclusive territory, per the 2025 FDD.

nationwide.note: US franchising program is national with no geographic restrictions published, but it is currently open only to internal candidates (at least 1 year as a Domino's general manager or supervisor)

liquidCapitalMin.note: Minimum liquid capital $75,000; net worth requirement $250,000

totalInvestmentMin.note: 2025 FDD range $156,450-$743,500; conflict: Franchise Direct's summary lists a $107,450 low — FranchiseChatter's direct 2025 FDD citation used

franchiseFee.note: 2025 FDD initial franchise fee ranges $0-$10,000 depending on store type/program (lower bound shown); some directories cite a $25,000 figure that does not match the FDD

adFundPercent.note: 4% of weekly royalty sales per 2025 FDD (corroborated by Franchise Direct); some directories cite 5%-8%

avgUnitRevenue.note: Derived: 2025 FDD Item 19 average weekly unit sales of $26,467 for franchised US stores (2024) x 52 weeks. 'Royalty sales' = total receipts excluding sales tax and coupon discounts; gross sales, not profit.

totalUnits.note: Global estimate; US year-end 2024 per 2025 FDD: 6,751 franchised + 292 company-owned = 7,043 stores

yearFounded.note: 'Domino's started out small, with just one store in Ypsilanti, Michigan, in 1960'; Franchise Direct lists 1963, franchisor's own history page used

item19Available.note: Item 19 discloses average weekly unit sales for franchised and company-owned US stores

exclusiveTerritories.note: Area of primary responsibility only (~1-mile radius; half-mile in dense areas)

absenteeOwnership.note: Store must always be under on-premises supervision of the franchisee or controlling person

canRunPartTime.note: Franchisee must devote full time as manager of the store
